query_normalized: statistical bias in pre-print server publication trends statistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) and (b) as it empirically measures a specific form of statistical bias (citation bias based on author affiliation) within the exact domain of pre-print servers (bioRxiv), providing essential evidence on how non-scholarly attributes skew publication impact metrics. While it focuses on citation bias rather than general publication trends, it establishes a critical baseline for the "bias" component of the user's question regarding pre-print server statistics.
